response.justification: The candidate brings 13+ years of broad, polyglot engineering experience including high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, millions of requests/sec), C/C++ measurement tooling at National Instruments, and a strong software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). However, the resume shows no direct experience with experimentation platforms, feature flagging, A/B testing, telemetry/metrics foundations, or statistical analysis — the core of this Principal/IC6 growth-and-experimentation role. Most titles are Senior/Staff at smaller orgs rather than Principal-level multi-team architectural leadership, and there is no evidence of the data aptitude or bias-detection/rollout-risk work the role emphasizes. Worth a screening conversation to probe transferable distributed-systems and measurement experience, but currently a domain mismatch.
strengths: 13+ years polyglot experience; proven high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems work
Hands-on C/C++ on measurement tooling plus strong TDD/CI-CD engineering fundamentals
Recent AI-agent infrastructure and developer-tooling exposure (MCP, agentic workflows)
concerns: No demonstrated experimentation-platform, feature-flagging, or A/B-testing experience
Lacks evidence of data/statistical aptitude, telemetry foundations, and bias/rollout-risk work
Career scope reads Senior/Staff, not Principal-level multi-team architectural leadership
